questions.question: A park ranger observed that a hiking trail had $15%$ more hikers this year than last year. If there were $240$ hikers last year, how many hikers are on the trail this year?
A fuel-efficient car can travel $25$ miles per gallon. If the price of fuel increases by $12%$, how much will it cost to travel $500$ miles if the original price was $$3.50$ per gallon?
A conservation effort resulted in a $30%$ increase in the population of a rare animal species. If there were originally $80$ animals, how many animals are there now?
A group of friends plan to hike a trail that is $16$ miles long. If they have already hiked $25%$ of the trail, how many miles do they still have to hike?
A wildlife sanctuary has $250$ acres of land. If $18%$ of the land is dedicated to a bird sanctuary, how many acres are dedicated to the birds?
A camper's backpack weighs $25$ pounds. If the weight of the backpack increases by $15%$ due to added gear, how much will the backpack weigh now?
A park's visitor center sells trail maps for $$5$ each. If the price of the maps increases by $20%$, how much will a visitor pay for $3$ maps?
A group of friends want to reduce their water consumption by $10%$. If they currently use $150$ gallons per day, how many gallons will they use after the reduction?

In a forest, the population of deer increased from $120$ to $150$ over a $2$-year period. What is the average annual percent increase in the deer population? Show your work and calculate the answer as a percent.

A hiker is planning a trip that is $30$ miles long. If she has already hiked $20%$ of the distance, what percent of the trip still remains? Additionally, if she hikes at a rate of $3$ miles per hour, how many hours will it take her to complete the remaining distance? Show your work and calculate both answers.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the concept of percentages and how to apply them to real-world problems.
Tip 2: Encourage students to show their work and explain their reasoning for each problem.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their units and ensure they are answering the question being asked.
